title: Test cases input for the openpyxl writer
description: Contract between the SKILL.md agent (producer) and write_test_cases.py (consumer). The agent emits this after the scope gate is approved. The writer NEVER invents content beyond what is here.

feature.description: Feature/module display name. Written to sheet C1, used to rename the sheet tab (sanitized, <=31 chars, unique), and shown as the Test Report module-code cell. The writer rewrites Test Report formula refs to the renamed tab, so renaming is safe.

language.description: Output language of the B/C/D/E cell text (test-case writing language, asked per run at Step 6). Distinct from the session communication language.

environment.description: Optional. Test Report Environment value (e.g. 'Web: Chrome, Safari'). Omit to leave blank for the human.

sections.description: Ordered sections. Each becomes a standalone B-only header row, followed by its case rows.

name.description: Section header text, written to column B on its own row (e.g. 'General interface', 'Image tab').

case.description: One logical test item. The first row carries the description (column B); subsequent rows leave B blank (continuation rows that vary precondition/procedure/expected).

description.description: Test Case Description (column B). Terse imperative, e.g. 'Check "Image" tab'. Written only on the FIRST row of this case.

rows.description: One or more rows under this description. rows[0] pairs with the description; rows[1..] are continuation rows (blank B).

viewpoint.description: METADATA ONLY — not written to Excel (template has no viewpoint column). Test viewpoint, e.g. function/performance/security/boundary. Lives in scope.md for coverage tracking.

type.description: METADATA ONLY — not written to Excel. Normal vs abnormal classification for coverage tracking.

precondition.description: Pre-Condition (column C). Sparse; often blank. Data-state variants like 'No data' / 'Have data'. Omit or empty string for blank.

procedure.description: Test Case Procedure (column D). Numbered '1. ... \n 2. ...' or freeform ('Check UI'). Use literal \n for line breaks; writer enables wrap-text.

expected.description: Expected Output (column E). Multiline, bulleted/numbered, concrete values. Use literal \n for line breaks. NOTE: column A auto-ID counts rows where E is non-empty, so every written row MUST have a non-empty expected.
